activities.learner_task: State what an enzyme is and what reaction rate means in this investigation context.
activities.expected_output: A short accurate definition pair without mechanism claim.
activities.activity_preamble: Before explaining causes, lock the basic vocabulary.

required_materials.purpose: Orient learners to enzyme and reaction-rate vocabulary.
required_materials.specification: Brief definitions only; no temperature mechanism.

activities.learner_task: Explain why reaction rate rises then falls as temperature increases within and beyond the enzyme's stable range.
activities.expected_output: A causal chain account connecting temperature change to rate outcome via intervening links.
activities.activity_preamble: You will explain the temperature–rate relationship as a transmitting process.

required_materials.purpose: Teach how temperature affects enzyme reaction rate through a causal chain.
required_materials.specification: Explain temperature→rate rise then fall with intervening links; natural prose only.

archetype_plan.start: temperature increases within and beyond the enzyme's stable range
archetype_plan.outcome: reaction rate first increases and then decreases
required_links: molecular kinetic energy and collision frequency
disruption of enzyme structure at high temperature

activities.learner_task: Build a working model of thermostat-controlled heating and use it to contrast mild vs extreme cold.
activities.expected_output: A coherent system model that explains both contrast states with the same relationships and constraint.
activities.activity_preamble: Hold a durable model; then apply it across states.

required_materials.purpose: Help learners assemble a durable working model of thermostat-controlled heating.
required_materials.specification: Relationship-and-constraint model that contrasts mild vs extreme cold; natural prose only.

archetype_plan.system: a room heated by a thermostat-controlled heater
key_relationships: the thermostat compares room temperature with the set point
the heater switches on when the room is below the set point and off when it reaches it
the room continually loses heat to colder surroundings
archetype_plan.governing_constraint: the heater has a finite heating capacity

contrast.state_a: the outside temperature is mildly cold
contrast.state_b: the outside temperature becomes extremely cold

activities.learner_task: Work through expert interpretation of an enzyme reaction-rate investigation from condition to conclusion.
activities.expected_output: A continuous walkthrough that transfers findings across stages to a bounded conclusion.
activities.activity_preamble: Model how an expert reads the evidence set.

required_materials.purpose: Model expert interpretation of an enzyme reaction-rate investigation.
required_materials.specification: Ordered process walkthrough from condition identification to bounded conclusion.

stages: identify the manipulated condition and measured outcome
connect the pattern to enzyme behaviour
